## Public / Private Boundary Check

- [ ] No secrets, credentials, tokens, private keys, or sensitive local paths.
- [ ] No customer, user, partner, or private operator data.
- [ ] No private strategy, internal coordination logs, or production-only details.
- [ ] No unverified public claims presented as verified facts.
- [ ] No HUMMBL brand, trademark, commercial-use, or hosted-service claim beyond documented boundaries.
- [ ] No prohibited vendor attribution strings.

## Claim Receipts

List any public claims added or changed and attach receipts. If no public claims
changed, write `None`.

| Claim | Status | Receipt |
|---|---|---|
|  |  |  |

## Risk

- [ ] Low: docs, comments, tests, or isolated examples only.
- [ ] Medium: package behavior, public API, CI, docs claims, release metadata, or MCP surface.
- [ ] High: security, governance policy, public claims, package publishing, branch protection, or external integrations.

## Rollback

-

## Reviewer Checklist

- [ ] Scope is bounded and reversible.
- [ ] Tests or docs verification are appropriate for the change.
- [ ] Claim receipts are sufficient.
- [ ] Public/private boundary is acceptable.
- [ ] License, trademark, brand, commercial-use, and hosted-service boundaries are respected.
- [ ] Security reporting or disclosure paths are not weakened.
